prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='CWT_rf', seasonality_champion='FT_catboost', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.367) and extreme seasonality variance (0.9285), indicating models capture patterns very differently. CWT_rf dominates overall with best trend tracking (slope_err=0.1264) and lowest RMSE, while FT_catboost excels at seasonality (corr=0.6773). Early/late horizon specialists differ significantly, supporting heterogeneous model combinations. DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating disagreement risks.

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.367) and extreme seasonality variance (0.9285), indicating models capture patterns very differently. CWT_rf dominates overall with best trend tracking (slope_err=0.1264) and lowest RMSE, while FT_catboost excels at seasonality (corr=0.6773). Early/late horizon specialists differ significantly, supporting heterogeneous model combinations. DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating disagreement risks.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates high horizon heterogeneity, favoring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread_ratio=0.367 suggests significant model disagreement, making dba_combination (recommended by pattern_analyst) essential for phase-shift robustness. n_windows=3 is low, so top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.35 provides stability. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures the best single model per horizon. The combination balances selection diversity with weighted averaging to mitigate disagreement risks.
